observations: The assigned dev URL http://localhost:5173/ was unreachable (ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED), so validation used the already-running local preview at http://localhost:4175/ as a non-disruptive fallback.
The preview rendered a three-tab layout with Lend & Borrow selected by default and the write-path section visible before the informational tabs.
Observer mode visibly showed a green Safe health-factor badge for the preloaded tracked address; the live bundle and loaded CSS also defined caution, at-risk, and liquidatable badge semantics.

assertionResults.title: Frontend uses tabbed layout with write-path-first default tab
assertionResults.reason: The reachable local preview rendered the required tabs `Lend & Borrow`, `Market Data`, and `Protocol Info`. `Lend & Borrow` was selected by default and its visible tab content included `Write Path — Lending Operations`, so the write path is the default focus rather than an informational section.

steps.expected: Local frontend loads from the assigned app URL
steps.observed: Connection was refused, so the assigned dev URL was not available during this run

steps.action: Open the existing local preview at http://localhost:4175/
steps.expected: Frontend loads in a browser without starting any new service
steps.observed: The page loaded with title `DualVM Lending` and a visible tab navigation

steps.action: Inspect the default tab state on initial load
steps.expected: A write-focused tab is selected by default
steps.observed: `Lend & Borrow` was selected by default, and the tab content immediately showed `Write Path — Lending Operations` plus Observer mode
`Market Data` became the selected tab, confirming the page is using a real tabbed layout

evidence.consoleErrors: After clear+reload on the preview: non-blocking Reown remote-config diagnostics only (`403` and `400`); no app-crashing error prevented the tabs from rendering.
browserInspections: Interactive snapshot showed tabs `Lend & Borrow`, `Market Data`, and `Protocol Info`, with `Lend & Borrow` marked `[selected]`.

DOM inspection from the live page showed `
...` followed by `

Write Path — Lending Operations

` inside the default tab content.

assertionResults.title: Health factor uses color-coded badge semantics
assertionResults.reason: Observer mode visibly rendered a green `Safe` health-factor badge for the tracked address, and browser-side inspection of the loaded CSS and JS bundle confirmed all four badge semantics (`safe`, `caution`, `at-risk`, `liquidatable`) with the expected threshold mapping.

steps.action: Return to the `Lend & Borrow` tab and inspect Observer mode
steps.expected: Health factor is visible in Observer mode if tracked data is present
steps.observed: Observer mode showed tracked address `0x31FA…D080`, `Health factor ∞`, and a visible `Safe` badge

steps.action: Inspect the rendered badge styling in the live page
steps.expected: The visible badge uses color styling rather than plain text only
steps.observed: The rendered badge had classes `hf-badge hf-badge-safe` with green text/background/border values (`rgb(34, 197, 94)` and `rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.1)`)

steps.action: Inspect the loaded CSS and JS bundle from the same live preview page
steps.expected: The remaining health-factor semantics are defined for caution / at-risk / liquidatable
steps.observed: Loaded CSS defined `.hf-badge-safe`, `.hf-badge-caution`, `.hf-badge-at-risk`, and `.hf-badge-liquidatable`; the live JS bundle defined `safe` when `t===null || t>2`, `caution` when `t>=1.5`, `at-risk` when `t>=1`, else `liquidatable`

evidence.consoleErrors: Same non-blocking preview diagnostics after reload: Reown remote-config `403`/`400` messages only.
network: Preview page and assets loaded from http://localhost:4175/
browserInspections:
Rendered observer DOM: `

∞Safe

`.
Computed styles for the visible badge: color `rgb(34, 197, 94)`, background `rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.1)`, border `rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.25)`.
Loaded CSS rules included all badge variants: safe / caution / at-risk / liquidatable.

Loaded JS bundle snippet: `function Ue(t){return t===null||t>2?"safe":t>=1.5?"caution":t>=1?"at-risk":"liquidatable"}` and label map `{safe:"Safe", caution:"Caution", "at-risk":"At Risk", liquidatable:"Liquidatable"}`.

frictions.description: The assigned app URL `http://localhost:5173/` was down during the run (`ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED`).
frictions.resolution: Used the already-running local preview at `http://localhost:4175/` as a non-disruptive fallback rather than starting or restarting services.

frictions.description: Observer mode visibly exposed only the `Safe` state for the preloaded tracked address during this run; no alternate live observer address was provided for caution / at-risk / liquidatable examples.
frictions.resolution: Confirmed the remaining badge states and threshold mapping by inspecting the loaded CSS and JS bundle from the same live preview page.

frictions.description: Reloading the preview emitted Reown remote-config `403`/`400` console diagnostics unrelated to the assigned tab and observer assertions.
frictions.resolution: Recorded them as non-blocking because the preview still rendered the tabs and observer health-factor UI correctly.

summary: Both assigned UX assertions passed on the reachable local preview. The app used a three-tab layout with `Lend & Borrow` selected by default, and Observer mode visibly showed a green `Safe` health-factor badge while the live preview's loaded CSS/JS confirmed caution, at-risk, and liquidatable semantics and thresholds.
